parsed.reason: REQ-001 matches SRC-001 approval, SRC-002 callback scope, SRC-003 policy fields for V-3001/RT-3001-N
REQ-001 matches SRC-001 and SRC-002, and SRC-003 confirms all required fields/policy conditions are satisfied.
Approved by SRC-001, callback record SRC-002 confirms scope per SRC-003 policy requirements.
SRC-003 requires callback_scope=destination_account_token for payment_rail_change; only contact_identity present (SRC-002) and approval is for mismatched action_type (SRC-001)

REQ-001 lacks the required callback_scope=destination_account_token; SRC-003 says this is mandatory, while SRC-001 is for vendor_profile_update and SRC-002 has callback_scope=contact_identity, so the records do not authorize this payment_rail_change.

Policy (SRC-003) requires a callback_scope=destination_account_token for action_type=payment_rail_change, but the only callback record provided (SRC-002) has scope=contact_identity, and the approval (SRC-001) is for a different action_type (vendor_profile_update), not the requested payment_rail_change. Missing required destination_account_token callback.

Eligibility approved (SRC-001) and hold cleared (SRC-002) satisfy the policy requirement (SRC-003) for payout_release of RF-3002; request (REQ-001) can proceed.
hold_status=active (SRC-002) violates SRC-003 requirement for hold_status=cleared (eligibility approved per SRC-001)
SRC-003 requires hold_status=cleared for payout_release, but SRC-002 shows hold_status=active despite SRC-001 approval.
Policy (SRC-003) requires hold_status=cleared, but SRC-002 shows hold_status=active for RF-3002, thus the condition for payout_release is not satisfied.

REQ-001 matches SRC-001 and SRC-002, and SRC-003 confirms all required conditions are satisfied for medication_activation.

All required policy elements are satisfied: case_id (C-3003) and med_plan (MED-3003) per REQ-001, scope_code=medication_activation per SRC-001, and pharmacy_check=accepted per SRC-002, matching the requirements outlined in SRC-003.
SRC-001 scope_code=scheduling_clearance mismatches SRC-003 policy requirement for scope_code=medication_activation (REQ-001, SRC-002 otherwise satisfied)
SRC-001 shows scope_code=scheduling_clearance, but SRC-003 requires scope_code=medication_activation along with pharmacy_check=accepted; the required scope is not satisfied.

Policy (SRC-003) requires scope_code=medication_activation and pharmacy_check=accepted. Pharmacy check is satisfied (SRC-002), but the clinical order shows scope_code=scheduling_clearance (SRC-001) with no record of medication_activation scope; missing required scope triggers escalation.

Policy (SRC-003) requires scope_code=transaction_execution and amount_limit ≥ 28600.00; SRC-001 provides scope_code=relationship_onboarding and amount_limit=not_applicable, violating both scope and amount conditions despite SRC-002 confirming relationship_status=current.

Incident commander approval (SRC-001) is recorded for sandbox only, not for the production environment required by the request (REQ-001); all other policy fields (endpoint, incident, environment, action, rollback_owner) are satisfied (SRC-002, SRC-003), but the missing production-level approval warrants escalation before execution.
